description: Massive Multitask Language Understanding. 57 subjects from STEM to humanities. Multiple-choice format testing breadth of knowledge.
name: MMLU

description: Enhanced MMLU with 10 answer choices, more reasoning-focused questions, and reduced sensitivity to prompt variation.
name: MMLU-Pro

description: Commonsense natural language inference. Choose the most plausible continuation of a scenario from adversarially-generated options.
name: HellaSwag

description: Grade-school science questions requiring reasoning. Challenge set contains questions that retrieval and co-occurrence methods fail on.
name: ARC (AI2 Reasoning Challenge)

description: Large-scale dataset of Winograd Schema Challenge problems. Fill-in-the-blank coreference resolution requiring commonsense reasoning.
name: WinoGrande

description: Expert-level questions in biology, physics, and chemistry designed to be unsearchable. Written by domain experts, validated by other experts.
name: GPQA (Graduate-Level Google-Proof QA)

description: Boolean (yes/no) question answering from naturally occurring queries paired with Wikipedia passages.
name: BoolQ

correctness_requirements: Human or model judge for truthfulness
description: Questions designed to elicit common misconceptions. Tests whether models reproduce falsehoods learned from training data.
limitations: Cultural bias in what counts as misconception
name: TruthfulQA

description: 23 challenging tasks from BIG-Bench where prior language models performed below average human raters. Tests multi-step reasoning.
limitations: Curated subset may not represent full difficulty spectrum
name: BBH (BIG-Bench Hard)

description: Discrete Reasoning Over Paragraphs. Reading comprehension requiring numerical reasoning, counting, sorting, and multi-hop inference.
name: DROP

description: Successor to GLUE benchmark with harder NLU tasks including reading comprehension, textual entailment, coreference, and word sense disambiguation.
name: SuperGLUE

description: Predict the last word of a passage requiring broad context understanding. Tests long-range dependency resolution.
name: LAMBADA

description: Reading Comprehension from English Examinations for Chinese middle and high school students. Multiple-choice comprehension questions.
name: RACE

description: Question Answering with Long Input Texts. Multiple-choice QA over documents averaging 5,000 words. Tests long-context comprehension.
name: QuALITY

description: Algorithmically generated complex reasoning problems requiring integration of information across multiple steps with natural language narratives.
name: MuSR (Multistep Soft Reasoning)

description: Instruction Following Evaluation. Tests whether models follow specific verifiable constraints in instructions (word count, format, inclusions).
name: IFEval

description: 164 hand-written Python programming problems with function signatures, docstrings, and unit tests. Tests functional correctness of generated code.
name: HumanEval

description: Augmented HumanEval with 80x more tests per problem. Catches solutions that pass original tests but are semantically incorrect.
gaming_vectors: Original HumanEval memorization still partially works
name: HumanEval+

description: 974 entry-level Python programming problems. Tests basic coding ability with natural language descriptions and test cases.
name: MBPP (Mostly Basic Python Problems)

correctness_requirements: Generated patch must pass repository test suite
description: Real-world software engineering tasks from GitHub issues. Models must generate patches to resolve actual issues in popular Python repositories.
name: SWE-bench

description: Human-verified subset of SWE-bench with confirmed solvable instances and validated test coverage.
name: SWE-bench Verified

correctness_requirements: Pass all test cases including hidden ones
description: Continuously updated benchmark from competitive programming platforms. New problems added regularly to prevent contamination.
limitations: Competitive programming style may not reflect real coding
name: LiveCodeBench

description: 10,000 coding problems from open-access coding platforms spanning introductory to competition level difficulty.
name: APPS

correctness_requirements: Pass all test cases including stress tests
description: Competitive programming problems from Codeforces with comprehensive test suites including generated tests.
name: CodeContests

correctness_requirements: Output matches expected or test assertions pass
description: 1,000 data science problems covering 7 popular Python libraries (NumPy, Pandas, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scipy, Sklearn, Matplotlib).
name: DS-1000

description: 800 Python functions testing code reasoning: predict output given input, and predict input given output.
name: CRUXEval

description: Translation of HumanEval and MBPP to 18 programming languages, enabling cross-language code generation evaluation.
limitations: Translated from Python, may not be idiomatic
name: MultiPL-E

description: Benchmark for AI coding assistants measuring ability to edit existing code across multiple languages based on natural language instructions.
name: Aider Polyglot

correctness_requirements: Final numerical answer must match exactly
description: 8,500 grade school math word problems requiring multi-step arithmetic reasoning. Each problem has a chain-of-thought solution.
name: GSM8K

description: 12,500 competition mathematics problems from AMC, AIME, and other competitions. Spans 7 subjects from algebra to number theory.
name: MATH
scale: 12,500 problems across 5 difficulty levels and 7 subjects

description: Curated 500-problem subset of MATH benchmark for efficient evaluation of mathematical reasoning capabilities.
name: MATH-500

description: Technical mathematical problems including calculus, linear algebra, and mathematical proof. Tests formal mathematical reasoning.
name: Minerva (MATH variant)

description: 800 questions requiring application of mathematical theorems from linear algebra, calculus, probability, and other areas.
name: TheoremQA

description: Problems from AIME competitions. Integer answers 0-999 requiring deep mathematical reasoning and creative problem-solving.
limitations: Very small sample size per year
name: AIME (American Invitational Mathematics Examination)
scale: ~15 problems per year, evaluated on recent years

description: Research-level mathematics problems created by professional mathematicians. Problems at the frontier of mathematical knowledge.
gaming_vectors: Very hard to game — novel problems
name: FrontierMath

description: 4,428 olympiad-level mathematical problems spanning all mathematical olympiad competitions worldwide.
name: OmniMATH

description: Autoformalization benchmark pairing informal mathematical statements with formal Lean proofs.
name: ProofNet

description: 13,679 crowdsourced science exam questions covering physics, chemistry, biology, and earth science.
name: SciQ

description: Biomedical question answering from PubMed abstracts. Yes/no/maybe answers requiring reasoning over research findings.
name: PubMedQA

description: Medical licensing exam questions (USMLE-style) testing clinical reasoning, diagnosis, and medical knowledge.
name: MedQA (USMLE)

description: STEM subset of MMLU covering abstract algebra, college biology, chemistry, physics, computer science, mathematics, and more.
name: MMLU-STEM

description: Interactive text-based environment for science experiments. Agents must perform multi-step procedures to demonstrate scientific understanding.
name: ScienceWorld

description: Comprehensive chemistry benchmark covering organic, inorganic, physical, and analytical chemistry at graduate level.
name: ChemBench

description: 11,500 multimodal questions from college exams requiring visual understanding across 30 subjects and 183 subfields.
name: MMMU (Massive Multi-discipline Multimodal Understanding)

description: Visual Question Answering benchmark with open-ended questions about images. Balanced pairs reduce language bias.
name: VQAv2

description: Mathematical reasoning in visual contexts. Tests understanding of charts, geometry, function plots, and scientific figures.
name: MathVista

description: Science diagrams with questions testing understanding of spatial relationships, processes, and scientific concepts depicted visually.
name: AI2D (AI2 Diagrams)

description: Question answering on document images. Tests OCR, layout understanding, and information extraction from real-world documents.
name: DocVQA

description: Question answering about charts and graphs requiring both visual parsing and reasoning about the depicted data.
name: ChartQA

description: Multi-modal evaluation of video understanding across diverse video types, durations, and question types.
name: Video-MME

description: Comprehensive audio understanding benchmark testing speech recognition, sound event detection, music understanding, and audio reasoning.
name: AudioBench

description: Standardized evaluation of language model safety against adversarial attacks. Tests resistance to jailbreaks and harmful content generation.
name: HarmBench

description: Machine-generated dataset for implicit hate speech detection. Tests models' ability to identify subtle toxicity across 13 minority groups.
name: ToxiGen

description: Evaluate social biases in question answering across 9 categories including age, gender, race, and religion.
name: BBQ (Bias Benchmark for QA)

description: Benchmark measuring model knowledge of dangerous information in biosecurity, cybersecurity, and chemical weapons as proxy for hazardous capabilities.
name: WMDP (Weapons of Mass Destruction Proxy)

description: Meta's cybersecurity evaluation testing models for insecure code generation, susceptibility to prompt injection, and cyber-attack assistance.
name: CyberSecEval

description: Evaluates AI agents' tendencies toward Machiavellian behavior (deception, manipulation, betrayal) in text-based game environments.
name: MACHIAVELLI

description: Comprehensive trustworthiness evaluation across 8 dimensions: toxicity, stereotype, adversarial robustness, OOD robustness, privacy, fairness, ethics, and machine ethics.
name: DecodingTrust

description: 100 critical safety test prompts that any responsible AI should refuse. A minimum bar for safety compliance.
name: SimpleSafetyTests

description: Realistic web environment benchmark. Agents complete complex tasks on self-hosted websites (shopping, forums, maps, GitLab).
name: WebArena

description: Operating system-level agent benchmark. Agents perform real computer tasks across Ubuntu, including file management, web browsing, and application use.
name: OSWorld

description: Real-world assistant tasks requiring tool use, web browsing, reasoning, and multi-step planning. Tests end-to-end capability.
name: GAIA (General AI Assistants)

description: Large-scale tool use benchmark with 16,000+ real-world REST APIs. Tests planning, API selection, and parameter filling.
name: ToolBench

description: Evaluates LLMs as agents across 8 environments: OS, database, knowledge graph, card game, lateral thinking, house-holding, web shopping, web browsing.
name: AgentBench

description: Evaluation framework for autonomous coding agents. Measures ability to navigate repos, identify bugs, and submit fixes autonomously.
name: SWE-agent

description: Multi-turn interaction benchmark testing tool-augmented LLMs on tasks requiring iterative reasoning with tools and feedback.
name: MINT (Multi-turn Interactive)

description: Evaluates language model agents on real-world tasks mimicking customer service interactions with tool use and policy compliance.
name: τ-bench (Tau-bench)

description: Industry-standard benchmark for ML training hardware/software performance. Measures time-to-train on reference models across multiple tasks.
name: MLPerf Training

description: Industry-standard benchmark for ML inference performance. Measures throughput and latency for serving trained models under various scenarios.
name: MLPerf Inference
scale: Multiple models and scenarios (offline, server, single-stream)

description: Efficiency metrics within Stanford HELM including inference cost, latency, and tokens per second across model comparisons.
name: HELM Efficiency
scale: Multiple models compared on same infrastructure

description: Large-scale human preference benchmark using blind pairwise comparisons. Elo ratings from crowd-sourced battles between anonymous models.
gaming_vectors: Verbose/polite answers win regardless of correctness
name: Chatbot Arena (LMSYS)

description: Automatic evaluator for instruction-following LLMs. Uses GPT-4 as judge to compare model outputs against reference (GPT-4/Claude).
name: AlpacaEval

description: Multi-turn conversation quality benchmark. GPT-4 judges responses across 80 multi-turn questions spanning 8 categories.
name: MT-Bench

description: Adversarial perturbations of GLUE tasks testing model robustness to word-level, sentence-level, and human-crafted attacks.
limitations: Attacks may not reflect real-world perturbations
name: AdvGLUE

description: Standardized adversarial robustness benchmark for image classifiers. Leaderboard tracks AutoAttack accuracy across threat models.
name: RobustBench
scale: CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, ImageNet with multiple threat models

description: Dynamic adversarial benchmark where humans iteratively craft examples that fool models, creating harder evaluation sets over time.
name: DynaBench

correctness_requirements: Pass rate on invariance and directional tests
description: Behavioral testing framework for NLP models using linguistic capabilities, invariance tests, and directional expectation tests.
name: CheckList
scale: User-defined test suites (thousands of generated tests)

correctness_requirements: Maintain original task performance under attack
description: Evaluates model susceptibility to various prompt injection attacks including direct injection, indirect injection, and jailbreaks.
name: Prompt Injection Benchmark

description: 204 diverse tasks probing language model capabilities beyond standard benchmarks. Contributed by 450+ authors covering logic, math, language, knowledge, and more.
name: BIG-Bench

description: Human-centric benchmark using real admission and qualification exams (SAT, LSAT, GRE, civil service exams, bar exams, etc.).
name: AGIEval
scale: 20 exams spanning multiple domains and languages

description: Visual pattern completion tasks testing fundamental abstraction and reasoning. Each task has unique rules that must be inferred from few examples.
gaming_vectors: Extremely hard to game — each task is unique
name: ARC-AGI (Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus)

description: Second iteration of ARC with harder tasks designed to remain challenging as AI capabilities improve. Focus on novel reasoning patterns.
name: ARC-AGI-2

description: Tests spatial-temporal reasoning that humans find trivial but AI often fails. Includes physical intuition, object tracking, and causal reasoning.
name: SIMPLE-Bench

description: Extremely challenging questions from domain experts across all fields. Designed to be the hardest possible evaluation of AI knowledge and reasoning.
gaming_vectors: Very hard to game — novel expert questions
name: Humanity's Last Exam

description: Comprehensive evaluation of long-context language models testing needle retrieval, variable tracking, aggregation, and question answering at various context lengths.
limitations: Synthetic tasks may not reflect real use
name: RULER
scale: Multiple tasks × multiple context lengths (4K to 128K+)

description: Tests ability to retrieve a specific piece of information inserted at various positions within long contexts of varying lengths.
gaming_vectors: Not meaningful beyond basic capability check
name: Needle in a Haystack

description: Bilingual multi-task benchmark for long-context understanding across 6 task categories with average length of 6K-20K tokens.
limitations: Moderate context lengths by current standards
name: LongBench

description: Benchmark requiring processing of 100K+ token contexts. Tests realistic long-document understanding beyond synthetic retrieval.
limitations: Few models can process full context
name: InfiniteBench

description: Translation of 250 GSM8K problems into 10 languages. Tests mathematical reasoning across language barriers.
name: MGSM (Multilingual GSM8K)

description: Multi-way parallel translation benchmark covering 200+ languages with human-translated evaluation sets.
name: FLORES

description: Cross-lingual evaluation covering 40 languages across 9 tasks including classification, QA, and structured prediction.
limitations: Task-specific training data varies by language
name: XTREME
